findings.evidence: The file advertises “compatibility with composition, inverse, and identity equivalences” at lines 24-26 and even declares unused `{L}` at line 43, but `rg` finds only `*_mk`, `*_symm_mk`, and coe lemmas; existing deck conjugation has the needed pattern at `Deck/Conjugation.lean:156` and `:165`.
findings.fix: Add `[simp]` characteristic lemmas for `normalizerEquivMap`/`normalizerQuotientEquivMap` under `MulEquiv.refl`, `.trans`, and `.symm`, with the required subgroup-map casts; mirror them for `Deck.normalizerQuotientConjEquiv` using `conjMulEquivRefl`/`conjMulEquivTrans`.
findings.issue: `normalizerQuotientEquivMap` has no identity/composition/symmetry compatibility lemmas, so downstream conjugacy bookkeeping cannot compose transports without unfolding/casts.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\].*normalizer"` reports `normalizerEquivMap` at line 47, `normalizerQuotientEquivMap` at line 90, and `Deck.normalizerQuotientConjEquiv` at `Deck/NormalizerQuotientConjugation.lean:43`, while representative formulas already exist at lines 55, 102, and 122.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from `normalizerEquivMap`, `normalizerQuotientEquivMap`, and `Deck.normalizerQuotientConjEquiv`; keep bodies opaque and provide the missing simp/functorial lemmas instead.
findings.issue: The new equivalence bodies are public via `@[expose]`, expanding the API surface and letting consumers depend on the chosen `QuotientGroup.congr`/`subgroupMap` implementation.

summary: The exposed transport maps are usable on representatives, but the public API is incomplete for the stated basepoint-change bookkeeping and exposes implementation bodies instead of supplying the missing characteristic lemmas.
